quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Angular momentum (rigid body)"?

options.text: L = Iω; the rotational analog of linear momentum, measured in kg·m²/s
I = ∫r² dm; requires integration over the mass distribution of the object
W = ∫F·dr; the scalar integral of force along displacement; measured in joules (J); positive when force and displacement are in the same direction
The contact force perpendicular to a surface, adjusting to prevent objects from passing through each other

quiz_questions.explanation: "Angular momentum (rigid body)" is defined as: L = Iω; the rotational analog of linear momentum, measured in kg·m²/s. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Angular momentum (particle)"?

options.text: L = r × p = r × mv; magnitude L = mvr sinθ; perpendicular distance times linear momentum
A mass on a string tracing a horizontal circle; T sinθ provides centripetal force while T cosθ balances gravity
The pulling force transmitted through a string, rope, or cable; in an ideal (massless) string, tension is the same throughout
x(t) = x₀ + ∫v(t)dt; integrating velocity recovers position, with the constant of integration set by initial conditions

quiz_questions.explanation: "Angular momentum (particle)" is defined as: L = r × p = r × mv; magnitude L = mvr sinθ; perpendicular distance times linear momentum. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Conservation of angular momentum"?

options.text: s = rθ; the arc length swept by a point at radius r when the object rotates through angle θ (in radians)
A force whose work depends only on initial and final positions, not the path taken (e.g., gravity, spring force); associated with potential energy
W_net = ½mv² - ½mv₀²; relates the total work done by all forces to the change in kinetic energy
When τ_net,ext = 0: I₁ω₁ = I₂ω₂; if I decreases, ω increases proportionally

quiz_questions.explanation: "Conservation of angular momentum" is defined as: When τ_net,ext = 0: I₁ω₁ = I₂ω₂; if I decreases, ω increases proportionally. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Torque-angular momentum relation"?

options.text: I = ∫r² dm; requires integration over the mass distribution of the object
F_net = ma or F_net = dp/dt; the net force equals mass times acceleration, or equivalently the rate of change of momentum
τ_net = dL/dt; the rotational analog of F = dp/dt
The gravitational force on an object is the vector sum of the gravitational forces from all other masses

quiz_questions.explanation: "Torque-angular momentum relation" is defined as: τ_net = dL/dt; the rotational analog of F = dp/dt. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Angular impulse"?

options.text: A system on which no net external force acts; internal forces cancel by Newton's Third Law
The change in position vector Δx = x_final - x_initial; a signed quantity indicating direction, measured in meters
E = KE + PE = ½mv² - GMm/r = -GMm/(2r) for circular orbits; negative means bound
∫τ dt = ΔL; the angular analog of linear impulse equals the change in angular momentum

quiz_questions.explanation: "Angular impulse" is defined as: ∫τ dt = ΔL; the angular analog of linear impulse equals the change in angular momentum. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "L = Iω; the rotational analog of linear momentum, measured in kg·m²/s"?

options.text: Angular momentum (rigid body)
Non-uniform circular motion
Instantaneous acceleration
Potential energy function

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Angular momentum (rigid body)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "L = r × p = r × mv; magnitude L = mvr sinθ; perpendicular distance times linear momentum"?

options.text: Center of mass reference frame
Angular momentum (particle)
Vertical circle dynamics
Common moments of inertia

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Angular momentum (particle)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "When τ_net,ext = 0: I₁ω₁ = I₂ω₂; if I decreases, ω increases proportionally"?

options.text: Restoring torque
Conservation of angular momentum
Free-body diagram
Total orbital energy

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Conservation of angular momentum".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "τ_net = dL/dt; the rotational analog of F = dp/dt"?

options.text: Friction as a non-conservative force
Torque-angular momentum relation
Potential energy
Cross product

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Torque-angular momentum relation".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "∫τ dt = ΔL; the angular analog of linear impulse equals the change in angular momentum"?

options.text: Angular impulse
Dot product
Elastic PE
Static friction

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Angular impulse".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Angular Momentum and Conservation is accurate?

options.text: For variable forces (such as springs: F = -kx), integration is essential: W_spring = ∫(-kx)dx = -½kx².
Angular Momentum and Conservation define angular momentum as L = Iω (for rotation about a fixed axis) or L = r × p (for a particle).
Torque about a pivot determines whether an object rotates and in which direction.
Dynamics connects force and motion through Newton's three laws.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Angular Momentum and Conservation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Angular Momentum and Conservation?

options.text: Newton's Second Law for rotation in momentum form gives τ_net = dL/dt.
Force-potential energy diagrams reveal equilibrium positions (where F = -dU/dx = 0) and their stability (slope determines restoring or repelling character).
Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM) occurs when the restoring force is proportional to displacement: F = -kx, producing sinusoidal oscillations x(t) = A cos(ωt + φ).
Vectors and Components provide the mathematical language for describing physical quantities with both magnitude and direction.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Angular Momentum and Conservation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Angular Momentum and Conservation?

options.text: The angular frequency ω = √(k/m) depends on system parameters (spring constant and mass), not amplitude.
Power and Efficiency describe the rate of energy transfer and the fraction of input energy converted to useful output.
Collisions are classified by whether kinetic energy is conserved.
When net external torque is zero, angular momentum is conserved: L_i = L_f (I₁ω₁ = I₂ω₂).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Angular Momentum and Conservation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Angular Momentum and Conservation?

options.text: The parallel axis theorem I = I_cm + Md² relates the moment of inertia about any axis to that about the center of mass.
AP Physics C emphasizes setting up and evaluating these integrals.
Conservation of angular momentum explains phenomena from spinning ice skaters to the formation of galaxies.
Multiple-force problems require careful free-body analysis at specific positions.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Angular Momentum and Conservation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Angular Momentum and Conservation?

options.text: Force-potential energy diagrams reveal equilibrium positions (where F = -dU/dx = 0) and their stability (slope determines restoring or repelling character).
Angular Momentum and Conservation define angular momentum as L = Iω (for rotation about a fixed axis) or L = r × p (for a particle).
In AP Physics C, the calculus formulation F = dp/dt can be applied to systems with time-varying forces, including rocket propulsion where mass changes continuously.
Dynamics connects force and motion through Newton's three laws.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Angular Momentum and Conservation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Angular Momentum and Conservation?

options.text: Calculating the mass of Earth from the known value of g at the surface and Earth's radius: M = gR²/G
Roller coaster engineering ensuring the car speed at the top of a loop exceeds √(gr) so riders maintain contact with their seats
Ice skater spins: decreasing I by pulling arms in causes ω to increase to conserve L; extending arms slows the spin
Analyzing the work required to stretch a bungee cord by integrating the non-linear elastic force over the extension distance

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Angular Momentum and Conservation.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Angular Momentum and Conservation?

options.text: Bridge engineering calculating torques about supports to determine forces on beams and ensure structural equilibrium
Stellar evolution: collapsing gas clouds spin faster as they shrink (I decreases → ω increases), explaining the rapid rotation of neutron stars
Molecular chemistry modeling interatomic bonds as potential energy curves (Lennard-Jones potential) to predict bond length and bond energy
Spacecraft docking maneuvers calculating the center of mass of the combined vehicle to plan thruster firings for orbit adjustments

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Angular Momentum and Conservation.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Angular Momentum and Conservation applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Roller coaster engineering ensuring the car speed at the top of a loop exceeds √(gr) so riders maintain contact with their seats
Helicopter tail rotor design: compensating for the angular momentum of the main rotor to prevent the helicopter body from spinning in the opposite direction
Vehicle accident reconstruction using partially inelastic collision models and coefficient of restitution to determine pre-collision speeds
Orbital mechanics where gravitational force provides the centripetal force: mg = mv²/r leads to orbital velocity v = √(gr) for low orbits

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Angular Momentum and Conservation.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Angular Momentum and Conservation?

options.text: Musical instrument design: string tension and density determine wave speed and thus the fundamental frequency of vibrating strings
Ice skater spins: decreasing I by pulling arms in causes ω to increase to conserve L; extending arms slows the spin
Structural engineering calculating forces on bridge components using free-body diagrams and Newton's laws to ensure safe load-bearing capacity
Civil engineering analyzing dam stability using gravitational potential energy and identifying stable vs. unstable equilibrium configurations

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Angular Momentum and Conservation.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Angular Momentum and Conservation in practice?

options.text: Earthquake-resistant building design using tuned mass dampers (large SHM systems) to counteract the building's oscillation and reduce structural damage
Ultrasound imaging using the wave equation v = λf to determine how ultrasound wave properties affect resolution and penetration depth in tissue
Stellar evolution: collapsing gas clouds spin faster as they shrink (I decreases → ω increases), explaining the rapid rotation of neutron stars
Earthquake engineering analyzing seismic wave propagation (P-waves longitudinal, S-waves transverse) through different geological layers

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Angular Momentum and Conservation.
